title: centaurea alba subsp. macrocephala
intro: Centaurea alba subsp. macrocephala, also known as the large-headed knapweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and polyacetylenes. The health benefits of Centaurea alba subsp. macrocephala include its potential to alleviate pain, reduce fever, and treat skin conditions such as acne and wounds. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine and folk remedies.
